糖分过多可能是心血管疾病的最大威胁之一。这是遏制你甜蜜习惯的方法。
   糖在健康方面享有盛誉。糖在所有含有碳水化合物的食物中都天然存在，例如水果和蔬菜，谷物和奶制品。食用含有天然糖的全食物是可以的。植物性食品还含有大量的纤维，必需矿物质和抗氧化剂，而乳制品则含有蛋白质和钙。
 由于您的身体消化这些食物的速度很慢，因此其中的糖分会为细胞提供稳定的能量供应。水果，蔬菜和全谷类食品的高摄入量也被证明可以降低患糖尿病，心脏病和某些癌症等慢性疾病的风险。
  但是，当您食用过多的糖时，就会出现问题，即食品制造商添加到产品中的糖会增加风味或延长保质期。
 在美国饮食中，主要来源是汽水，果汁饮料，调味酸奶，谷物，饼干，蛋糕，糖果和大多数加工食品。但是，在汤里，面包，腌肉和番茄酱等您可能不会想到的甜味食品中也添加了糖。
 结果：我们消耗了过多的糖。根据美国国家癌症研究所的数据，成年男性平均每天摄入24茶匙糖。那等于384卡路里。
 糖对肥胖症和糖尿病的影响已有充分文献记载，但令男人惊讶的一个方面是糖的口味如何严重影响他们的心脏健康，＆＃34;哈佛大学营养学教授弗兰克·胡（Frank Hu）博士说陈公共卫生学院。 
胡博士及其同事在2014年发表于《美国医学会内科学》（JAMA Internal Medicine）上的一项研究中，发现高糖饮食与心脏病致死的风险增加之间存在关联。在为期15年的研究过程中，从卡路里中摄取热量的17％至21％的人死于心血管疾病的风险要比在卡路里中摄取8％的人高38％。
 ＆＃34;基本上，添加糖的摄入量越高，患心脏病的风险越高，＆＃34;胡医生说。
 糖实际上如何影响心脏健康尚不完全清楚，但它似乎有几个间接联系。例如，大量糖分会使肝脏超负荷。 ＆＃34;肝脏与酒精一样通过糖代谢，并将饮食中的碳水化合物转化为脂肪，＆＃34;胡医生说。随着时间的流逝，这会导致更多的脂肪堆积，这可能会导致脂肪肝疾病，这是糖尿病的诱因，这会增加您患心脏病的风险。
 摄入过多的糖分会导致血压升高并加剧慢性炎症，这两种疾病都是导致心脏病的病理途径。糖的摄入过多，尤其是含糖饮料中的糖，也会通过诱使您的身体关闭其食欲控制系统而导致体重增加，因为液体卡路里不如固体食物中的卡路里令人满意。这就是为什么人们在食用含糖饮料时更容易在日常饮食中添加更多卡路里。
 ＆＃34;摄入糖分增加的影响-高血压，发炎，体重增加，糖尿病和脂肪肝疾病-都与心脏病发作和中风的风险增加有关，胡医生说。
  如果每天添加24茶匙糖过多，那么正确的量是多少？很难说，因为糖不是饮食中必需的营养素。设置推荐饮食津贴或RDA的医学研究所尚未发布糖的正式编号。
 但是，美国心脏协会（American Heart Association）建议，男性每天摄入的糖不超过150卡路里（约9茶匙或36克）。这接近于12盎司罐装苏打水的量。 
阅读食品标签是监控您摄入的糖分的最佳方法之一。查找添加糖的以下名称，并尝试避免或减少发现它们的食物的数量或频率：
  总糖（包括添加的糖）通常以克列出。注意每份糖的克数以及份数。 ＆＃34;每餐只能说5克糖，但是如果正常量是三或四份，则可以很容易地食用20克糖，因此可以大量添加糖，＆＃34;胡医生说。
 此外，还要跟踪添加到食物或饮料中的糖分。大约一半的添加糖来自饮料，包括咖啡和茶。 2017年5月《公共卫生》中的一项研究发现，大约三分之二的咖啡饮用者和三分之一的茶饮者将糖或含糖调味剂加入其饮料中。研究人员还指出，饮料中60％以上的卡路里来自糖分。
 然而，胡博士警告您不要为了减少糖分而大张旗鼓，因为这样做会适得其反。 ＆＃34;您可能会发现自己想购买其他食品来满足自己的甜味，例如精制淀粉（例如白面包和白米）会增加葡萄糖含量，而安慰富含饱和脂肪和钠的食物也会引起问题心脏健康，＆＃34;他说。

Too much added sugar can be one of the greatest threats to cardiovascular disease. Here's how to curb your sweet habit.
   Sugar has a bittersweet reputation when it comes to health. Sugar occurs naturally in all foods that contain carbohydrates, such as fruits and vegetables, grains, and dairy. Consuming whole foods that contain natural sugar is okay. Plant foods also have high amounts of fiber, essential minerals, and antioxidants, and dairy foods contain protein and calcium.
 Since your body digests these foods slowly, the sugar in them offers a steady supply of energy to your cells. A high intake of fruits, vegetables, and whole grains also has been shown to reduce the risk of chronic diseases, such as  diabetes, heart disease, and some cancers.
  However, problems occur when you consume too much  added sugar — that is, sugar that food manufacturers add to products to increase flavor or extend shelf life.
 In the American diet, the top sources are soft drinks, fruit drinks, flavored yogurts, cereals, cookies, cakes, candy, and most  processed foods. But added sugar is also present in items that you may not think of as sweetened, like soups, bread, cured meats, and ketchup.
 The result: we consume way too much added sugar. Adult men take in an average of 24 teaspoons of added sugar per day, according to the National Cancer Institute. That's equal to 384 calories.
 "Excess sugar's impact on obesity and diabetes is well documented, but one area that may surprise many men is how their taste for sugar can have a serious impact on their heart health," says Dr. Frank Hu, professor of nutrition at the Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health.
  In a study published in 2014 in  JAMA Internal Medicine, Dr. Hu and his colleagues found an association between a high-sugar diet and a greater risk of dying from heart disease. Over the course of the 15-year study, people who got 17% to 21% of their calories from added sugar had a 38% higher risk of dying from cardiovascular disease compared with those who consumed 8% of their calories as added sugar.
 "Basically, the higher the intake of added sugar, the higher the risk for heart disease," says Dr. Hu.
 How sugar actually affects heart health is not completely understood, but it appears to have several indirect connections. For instance, high amounts of sugar overload the liver. "Your liver metabolizes sugar the same way as alcohol, and converts dietary carbohydrates to fat," says Dr. Hu. Over time, this can lead to a greater accumulation of fat, which may turn into fatty liver disease, a contributor to diabetes, which raises your risk for heart disease.
 Consuming too much added sugar can raise blood pressure and increase chronic  inflammation, both of which are pathological pathways to heart disease. Excess consumption of sugar, especially in sugary beverages, also contributes to weight gain by tricking your body into turning off its appetite-control system because liquid calories are not as satisfying as calories from solid foods. This is why it is easier for people to add more calories to their regular diet when consuming sugary beverages.
 "The effects of added sugar intake — higher blood pressure, inflammation, weight gain, diabetes, and  fatty liver disease — are all linked to an increased risk for heart attack and stroke," says Dr. Hu.
  If 24 teaspoons of added sugar per day is too much, then what is the right amount? It's hard to say, since sugar is not a required nutrient in your diet. The Institute of Medicine, which sets Recommended Dietary Allowances, or RDAs, has not issued a formal number for sugar.
 However, the American Heart Association suggests that men consume no more than 150 calories (about 9 teaspoons or 36 grams) of added sugar per day. That is close to the amount in a 12-ounce can of soda.
  Reading food labels is one of the best ways to monitor your intake of added sugar. Look for the following names for added sugar and try to either avoid, or cut back on the amount or frequency of the foods where they are found:
  Total sugar, which includes added sugar, is often listed in grams. Note the number of grams of sugar per serving as well as the total number of servings. "It might only say 5 grams of sugar per serving, but if the normal amount is three or four servings, you can easily consume 20 grams of sugar and thus a lot of added sugar," says Dr. Hu.
 Also, keep track of sugar you add to your food or beverages. About half of added sugar comes from beverages, including coffee and tea. A study in the May 2017  Public Health found that about two-thirds of coffee drinkers and one-third of tea drinkers put sugar or sugary flavorings in their drinks. The researchers also noted that more than 60% of the calories in their beverages came from added sugar.
 Yet, Dr. Hu warns against being overzealous in your attempts to cut back on added sugar, as this can backfire. "You may find yourself reaching for other foods to satisfy your sweet cravings, like refined starches, such as white bread and white rice, which can increase glucose levels, and comfort foods high in saturated fat and sodium, which also cause problems with heart health," he says.

吉米·加利根（Jimmy Galligan）是来自弗吉尼亚州利斯堡的18岁大学新生。他也可能是取消克里斯托山伯爵文化的人。
 几个月前，混血儿加利甘（Galligan）发布了几年的消息。白人女同学种族歧视的三秒钟视频。加里根在视频上坐了很长时间，等待它造成最大破坏的那一刻。在那个女孩（一个叫Mimi Groves的啦啦队长）被田纳西大学录取之后，时间到了。
 ＆＃34;我想让她了解她对这个词的严重程度，＆＃34;加利甘说。
 该视频描述了当时15岁的格罗夫斯（Groves）并刚刚获得了学习许可，并说我可以开车，[渣浆]。这个话不是特别针对任何人的。带有该视频的简短视频剪辑在Snapchat上流传，直到被Galligan获取并保存为止，他对自己多久听到一次使用N字的白人同学感到愤怒。
 Galligan于6月公开分享了它。作为回应，格罗夫斯在UT的啦啦队中失去了位置。然后大学迫使她完全退出学校。招生办公室显然已经收到了来自愤怒的校友的数百条要求血液的信息。格罗夫斯现在正在上一所社区大学。
 这个故事是几种社会现象的有力例证：社会正义行动主义的好战行为，当今青少年的天真行为以及他们并未真正消失的Snapchat消息，社交媒体对暴民正义的渴望以及甚至高中生中一直存在的精心残酷的能力。但是，有关此事件的最疯狂的事情是，大多数人将通过阅读《纽约时报》来了解它。
 ＆＃34;种族辱骂，病毒式录像和推算。＆＃34;那是《泰晤士报》关于这个主题的文章的标题，该文章发表于圣诞节后的第二天。记者丹·莱文（Dan Levin）试图通过详细介绍格罗夫斯（Groves）和加里甘（Galligan）参加的Heritage High School令人不愉快的历史来增加相当大的背景。它坐落在一个富裕的，以白人为主的县，曾经在法院的场地上举行过奴隶拍卖。 
＆＃34;在访问中，有色人种的在校生和以前的学生都描述了一种种族不敏感的环境盛行，其中包括随意使用侮辱，＆＃34;莱文指出。 ＆＃34;学区去年委托的一份报告记录了学校领导的一种模式，这种模式忽视了学生和老师广泛使用种族歧视言论，从而培养了越来越感到绝望在有色人种的学生中，与白人学生相比，其中一些人面临不成比例的纪律措施。＆＃34;
 莱文将受委屈的学生的强烈抗议与更广泛的黑人生活问题运动联系起来，并在今年夏天乔治·弗洛伊德（George Floyd）和布罗娜·泰勒（Breonna Taylor）死于警方之后发生了抗议活动。但是他的文章却没有一个非常基本的事实：《纽约时报》选择协助一名少年的绝望追求，以破坏一名年轻女子的生命，该女子在15岁时就说了些愚蠢的话。
 大约25岁以上的每个人都应该感谢幸运星，他们在社交媒体和无处不在的拍照手机时代之前就完成了青春期，因为该国最重要的报纸显然认为，让青少年羞辱青少年行为是适当的。这是取消文化的最糟糕的方面-强烈的愿望是要让人们对自己在小时候犯的错误负责，即使他们早已学习了教训并越过了他们-时代已经完全接受了它。
 虽然这件作品力求中立，但显然它使加利根（Larry Galligan）狮子化了，他的肖像-出现在故事​​的早期-让我想起《华盛顿邮报》对莱西·格鲁伯（Lexie Gruber）和抒情王子（Lyric Prince）的奉承照片，他们将纸张勒索成幽默他们与万圣节服装有关的不满。列文从未真正挑战过加利甘。实际上，记者让加利根（Galligan）摆脱了他的白人父亲遭受“白人特权”的断言。格罗夫斯有些同情，但莱文确实应该解释了使用单词作为义词和以格罗夫斯的方式使用它之间的区别。
 或者更好的是，他可能根本不会写这个故事，这个故事令人担忧，但绝不是少见的少年行为。如果格罗夫斯在数学测试中作弊，或者在对手的背上插上了踢我的手势，这是否构成全国新闻？没有犯罪行为；这个单词的发音甚至没有在学校发生。关于这种情况的唯一新颖的地方是它引起了国家媒体的关注。
 出于这个原因，我不同意《美国保守党》中Rod Dreher的结论，后者将加里根描述为``道德上的怪物''。
  加利根做了一件可怕的事，但是我们每个人都不应该假装知道他是否是怪物。将他注销为不可挽回的坏事是不公平的，就像将格罗夫斯打成种族主义者并由于一个错误而破坏她的未来计划是不公平的。他们都是十几岁的年轻人，而且十几岁的年轻人（甚至那些最终被证明是非常优秀和正直的成年人）彼此之间确实做得很糟糕。 （也许您不记得高中了吗？我知道！）应该更正，原谅并允许他们继续前进。 
这就是为什么这种将青少年减少到生命中最糟糕时刻的新动力具有如此有害的病理学意义。它与大多数年轻人的情感和社交之旅完全不符。我们中只有极少数人像圣人一样在高中时期航行过，但实际上，今天的孩子们从12岁起就必须变得完美。
 真正应该知道更多的人不是这个故事的少年主题，而是《纽约时报》的编辑。想象一下，认为这篇论文不应该由在任的共和党参议员来反对，因为他的政策建议会使人们感到不安全，而是一个关于一个十几岁的女孩说些蠢话的故事？向她释放社交媒体的正义热情：1793年计划继续进行。

Jimmy Galligan is an 18-year-old college freshman from Leesburg, Virginia. He may also be cancel culture's Count of Monte Cristo.
 Some months ago, Galligan—who is biracial—posted a years' old, three-second video of a white, female classmate using a racial slur. Galligan had sat on the video for a long time, waiting for the moment it would do the most damage. After the girl—a cheerleader named Mimi Groves—was accepted to the University of Tennessee, the time had come.
 "I wanted to get her where she would understand the severity of that word," said Galligan.
 The video depicted Groves, who was 15 at the time, and had just obtained her learner's permit, saying "I can drive, [slur]." The remark was not directed at anyone in particular. The brief video clip featuring it circulated on Snapchat until it was obtained and saved by Galligan, who had grown furious at how often he heard his white classmates using the N-word.
 Galligan shared it publicly in June. In response, Groves lost her spot on UT's cheerleading squad. Then the university pressured her to withdraw from the school entirely. The admissions office had apparently received hundreds of messages from irate alumni demanding blood. Groves is now attending a community college.
 This story is a powerful example of several social phenomena:  the militant streak in social justice activism, the naivety of today's teens and their not-actually-disappearing Snapchat messages, social media's hunger for mob justice, and even the capacity for elaborate cruelty that has always existed among high schoolers. But the wildest thing about this incident is that most people will learn about it  by reading  The New York Times.
 "A Racial Slur, a Viral Video, and a Reckoning." That's the title of the  Times's article on the subject, published the day after Christmas. Reporter Dan Levin tries to add considerable context by detailing a history of alleged unpleasantness at Heritage High School, which Groves and Galligan attended. It sits in a wealthy, predominantly white county where "slave auctions were once held on the courthouse grounds."
 "In interviews, current and former students of color described an environment rife with racial insensitivity, including casual uses of slurs," notes Levin. "A report commissioned last year by the school district documented a pattern of school leaders ignoring the widespread use of racial slurs by both students and teachers, fostering a ' growing sense of despair' among students of color, some of whom faced disproportionate disciplinary measures compared with white students."
 Levin connects the outcry from aggrieved students to the broader Black Lives Matter movement and protests that occurred this summer following the deaths of George Floyd and Breonna Taylor at the hands of police. But nowhere does his article reckon with a very basic fact:  The New York Times has opted to assist a teenager's desperate quest to ruin the life of a young woman who said something stupid when she was 15.
 Everyone roughly 25 and older should thank their lucky stars that they completed adolescence before the age of social media and ubiquitous camera phones, because the country's most important newspaper apparently thinks it is appropriate to shame teenagers over their juvenile behavior. This is the very worst aspect of cancel culture—the burning desire to hold people accountable for mistakes they made as kids, even if they have long since learned their lesson and grown past them—and the  Times has fully embraced it.
 While the piece strives for a veneer of neutrality, it clearly lionizes Galligan, whose portrait—which appears early in the story—calls to mind  The Washington Post's excessively flattering photograph of Lexie Gruber and Lyric Prince, who  extorted the paper into humoring their Halloween-costume-related grievance. Levin never really challenges Galligan; in fact, the reporter lets Galligan get away with the assertion that his white father suffers from "white privilege." Groves is treated somewhat sympathetically, but Levin really should have explained the difference between using the word as an epithet and using it in the manner Groves did.
 Or better yet, he could have simply not written this story, which concerns bad but by no means uncommon teenager behavior. If Groves had cheated on her math test, or planted a kick-me sign on a rival's back, would this constitute national news? No crime was committed; the utterance of the word did not even take place at school. The only thing novel about this situation is that it attracted the national media's attention.
 It's for this reason that I do not share the conclusion of Rod Dreher in  The American Conservative, who described Galligan as a "moral monster."
  Galligan did a monstrous thing, but none of us should pretend to know whether he is a monster. It's unfair to write him off as irredeemably bad, just as it was unfair to brand Groves a racist and derail her future plans because of one mistake. They are both teenagers, and teenagers—even ones who turn out to be perfectly fine and upstanding adults—do really terrible things to each other. (Maybe you don't remember high school? I do!) They should be corrected, forgiven, and allowed to move on.
 That's why this new drive to  reduce teens to the worst moment of their lives is so pathologically toxic. It's completely at odds with the emotional and social journeys of most young people. Very few of us sailed through high school as saints, but today's kids are practically required to be perfect  from the time they turn 12.
 The people who really ought to have known better are not the story's teenage subjects, but its editors at  The New York Times. Imagine thinking the paper should not run an  op-ed by a sitting Republican senator because his policy proposal makes people feel unsafe, but a story about a teenage girl who said something stupid? Unleash the righteous fervor of social media upon her:  The 1793 Project continues.

His transgression was done with deliberate forethought, after reaching the age of majority, and for the express purpose of inflicting harm for a long held grudge. He is the toxic individual who will find himself untouchable by anyone finding his name on an internet search. He has shown himself to be an unstable ticking time bomb for any university, employer, landlord, or anyone else who might come into contact with him.
 I don’t even see where the article claims she used the “hard R”, in contrast she claims it was from the rap music they used to listen to:  Ms. Groves said the video began as a private Snapchat message to a friend. “At the time, I didn’t understand the severity of the word, or the history and context behind it because I was so young,” she said in a recent interview, adding that the slur was in “all the songs we listened to, and I’m not using that as an excuse.”
 The word is used by a popular entertainment culture and people are surprised when kids use it?
  It seemed pretty obvious the NYTimes had been taken over by a certain childish, illiberal mindset after staff reaction to Bari Weiss.
 Bari needs to pop out those Khazar milkers and bring all the goys to the yard.

一百多年前，一位名叫约尔根·布朗伦德（JørgenBrønlund）的丹麦探险家在格陵兰东北部探险队中与两名探险队成员一起丧生。他留下了日记，详细描述了自己的最后时刻，在他的最后签名下有一个黑点。根据发表在《考古学》杂志上的11月论文，科学家们现在已经使用多种技术分析了该斑点，以确定其组成，从而在布伦德的最后几个小时里散发出了新鲜光。
 格陵兰东北部仍然是北极最敌对的地区之一，只有丹麦军的小天狼星巡逻队在一年中最冷的时候偶尔越过冰冻的雪橇在狗的雪橇上。早在1906年，丹麦远征军发射升空时，该地区的许多地区尚未绘制地图。那是远征队的主要目标，以及各种科学研究。 （阿尔弗雷德·韦格纳是探险队的科学家之一。）
 探险队乘SS丹马克号航行到格陵兰，于1906年8月登陆并建立了一个名为Danmarkshavn的基地营（仓库）。成员被分配到雪橇队向北前进。约尔根·布朗伦（JørgenBrønlund）与远征军司令卢德维格·迈利乌斯·埃里克森（Ludvig Mylius-Erichsen）和尼尔斯·彼得·霍格·哈根（Niels PeterHøegHagen）一起是雪橇1号队的成员。他们任务的重要部分是发现所谓的Peary Land（由罗伯特·皮瑞（Robert Peary）在1891年发现）是一个半岛（在这种情况下仍将是丹麦王国的一部分）或一个岛屿，在这种情况下，美国将声称它是美国领土。
  1907年3月，雪橇车队向北行驶，受到冰块屈曲和破裂以及雪橇损坏的阻碍。 Mylius-Erichsen，Brønlund和HøegHagen决定沿海岸线行进，但由于那条路线意外地将他们引向东北，因此指挥官越来越担心。随着时间和条款的缩短，这意味着离他们的最终目标还有一段距离。当他和他的两个雪橇伴侣进入未映射的丹马克峡湾时，事实证明这是一条死胡同，迫使他们向东北退回，回到峡湾口，浪费了宝贵的时间。融化的冰将使人们无法返回丹马克沙芬，他们不想在没有足够食物和供应的情况下陷在该地区。
    那是Mylius-Erichsen做出的决定性的决定，不返回战舰，而是向西行驶，以期到达独立峡湾首领的海军悬崖。到了这个时候，夏天的融雪开始了，狩猎条件很差，加上他们的鞋子由于在石质地面上徒步旅行而磨损了。简而言之，正如布朗伦（Brønlund）在他的日记中所指出的那样，他们的处境非常糟糕：没有食物，没有脚齿轮，离船只有几百英里。我们的前景确实非常糟糕。＆＃34;
 到了9月，他们被迫尝试穿越79峡湾的冰冻水域。他们只剩下一只雪橇和四只狗。霍格·哈根（HøegHagen）于1907年11月15日因精疲力竭而首先去世，十天后Mylius-Erichsen逝世。实际上，布朗伦（Brønlund）到达了峡湾的另一侧，到达了探险队现有的一个仓库中。但是他脚下的黑暗和严厉的冻伤使他无法前进。他在一个山洞里避难并写下了他的最后日记：
 在11月返回内陆冰川的艰辛旅程中，我在北纬77度遇难。由于脚冻和黑暗，我在月亮渐弱的情况下到达了这个地方，无法继续前进。其他人的尸体在峡湾中部。大约十天后，哈根于11月15日去世，Mylius-Erichsen死了。 
次年春天，另外两名探险队成员发现了布隆伦德的遗体，日记安全地存放在他脚下的雪橇上。他们将他埋葬在他死的地方，今天的岬角被称为布隆伦德坟墓。至于其余的丹麦探险队，则取得了显著成就，发表了约51篇报道，尽管这一成功被布伦德（Brønlund），玛格丽特·埃里克森（Mylius-Erichsen）和霍格·哈根（HøegHagen）的惨死所掩盖。
 这份长达172页的日记目前存放在哥本哈根的皇家图书馆中。布伦隆德的签名下面是一个看起来很奇怪的黑点。 1993年，读者用小刀将其移除。 ＆＃34;这是在未经许可的情况下完成的，这当然是非正统的和非法的，＆＃34;作者在论文中指出。幸运的是，该样品最终归丹麦国家博物馆的自然科学部门所有，但尽管经过了许多专家的审查，仍无法得出有关材料可能是什么的结论。
     这就是当前研究的目的。早在2018年，Kaare Lund Rasmussen和南丹麦大学的几位同事与国家博物馆和皇家图书馆合作，对样品的成分做出了确定的确定。该小组从黑点中抽取了少量样品，并通过X射线荧光，同步辐射粉末X射线衍射，拉曼光谱和气相色谱质谱法等对其进行了分析。
 他们发现该地点由燃烧的橡胶，植物油，鱼类或动物脂肪，石油和粪便组成。还有针铁矿，这是环境中常见的一种矿物。烧焦的橡胶很可能来自Brønlund在他的补给品中使用的勒克斯燃烧器中的垫圈，并且可能是由于注定的探险家努力使用可用的任何易燃材料来点燃火以保暖而被烧掉的。 （燃烧器是在1973年由Sirius Patrol回收的。）作者推测，Brønlund死前大约五到六天就在山洞里，因为雪橇盒几乎是半空的。
 ＆＃34;这种新知识为Brønlund的最后几个小时提供了独特的见解，拉斯穆森说。 ＆＃34;我看到了他，如何变得虚弱，握着肮脏的握手，摸索着试图点燃燃烧器，但失败了。他必须找到其他东西才能使燃烧器运转。您可以使用纸张或上油的织物，但这很困难。我们认为他尝试了可用的油脂，因为黑点中含有微量的植物油脂以及可能来自鱼类，动物或蜡蜡烛的油脂。＆＃34;
note_en:
Over 100 years ago, a Danish explorer named  Jørgen Brønlund perished during  an expedition to northeast Greenland, along with two members of his expedition. He left behind a diary detailing his last moments, with a black spot underneath his final signature. Scientists have now analyzed that spot using a variety of techniques to determine its composition, thereby shedding fresh light on Brønlund's final hours, according to  a November paper published in the journal Archaeometry.
 Northeast Greenland is still one of the most hostile regions of the Arctic, with only the  Sirius Patrol of the Danish Army occasionally crossing the frozen expanse on dog sledges during the coldest part of the year. Back in 1906, when the Denmark Expedition launched, many parts of the region had not yet been mapped; that was a primary objective of the expedition, along with various scientific studies. (Alfred Wegener was among the scientists in the expedition.)
 The expedition sailed to Greenland on board the  SS Danmark, landing in August 1906 and establishing a base camp (depot) called Danmarkshavn. Members were assigned to sledge teams to head northward. Jørgen Brønlund was part of Sledge Team 1, along with expedition commander Ludvig Mylius-Erichsen and Niels Peter Høeg Hagen. A significant part of their mission was to discover whether the so-called Peary Land (discovered by Robert Peary in 1891) was a peninsula—in which case it would remain part of the Danish Kingdom—or an island, in which case the US would claim it as a US territory.
  The sledge teams headed north in March 1907, hampered by buckling and cracking ice as well as broken-down sledges. Mylius-Erichsen, Brønlund, and Høeg Hagen decided to follow the shoreline, but the commander became increasingly concerned as that route unexpectedly led them northeast. That meant a greater distance to their ultimate goal, as time and provisions grew short. When he and his two sledge mates entered the unmapped Danmark Fjord, it turned out to be a dead end, forcing them to backtrack northeast to return to the mouth of the fjord—wasting precious time. Melting ice would make passage back to Danmarkshavn impossible, and they didn't want to get stuck in the area without sufficient food and supplies.
    That's when Mylius-Erichsen made the fateful decision not to return to the ship, but to head west in hopes of reaching Navy Cliff at the head of Independence Fjord. By this time the summer thaw had set in, and hunting conditions were poor, plus their shoes had worn through from trekking over the stony ground. In short, as Brønlund noted in his diary, their situation was dire: "no food, no foot gear, and several hundred miles to the ship. Our prospects are very bad indeed."
 By September, they were forced to try to cross the frozen waters of the 79-Fjord. They were down to one sledge and four dogs. Høeg Hagen died first, from exhaustion, on November 15, 1907, with Mylius-Erichsen passing ten days later. Brønlund actually made it to the other side of the fjord, to one of the expedition's existing depots. But the darkness and severe frostbite in his feet prevented him from going any further. He took shelter in a cave and wrote his final diary entry:
 I perished in 77° N lat., under the hardships of the return journey over the inland ice in November. I reached this place under a waning moon, and cannot go on, because of my frozen feet and the darkness. The bodies of the others are in the middle of the fjord. Hagen died on November 15, Mylius-Erichsen some ten days later.
 The following spring, two other expedition members found Brønlund's body, with the diary safely stored in the sledge at his feet. They buried him where he died, and the headland is known today as Brønlund's Grave. As for the rest of the Danish Expedition, it was remarkably successful—some 51 reports were published—although that success was overshadowed by the tragic deaths of Brønlund, Mylius-Erichsen, and Høeg Hagen.
 The 172-page diary is currently housed in the Royal Library in Copenhagen. Below Brønlund's signature was an odd-looking black spot. In 1993, the spot was removed with a pocket knife by a reader. "This was done without permission which is of course both unorthodox and illegal," the authors noted in their paper. Fortunately, the sample ended up with the National Museum of Denmark's Natural Science Unit, but despite being examined by many experts, no conclusions could be drawn about what the material might be.
     That's where the current study comes in. Back in 2018, Kaare Lund Rasmussen and several colleagues at the University of Southern Denmark collaborated with the National Museum and Royal Library to make a definitive determination of the sample's composition. The team took small samples from the black spot and analyzed them with X-ray fluorescence, synchrotron radiation powder x-ray diffraction, Raman spectroscopy, and gas chromatography mass spectrometry, among other methods.
 They discovered that the spot was made up of burnt rubber, vegetable oil, fish or animal fats, petroleum, and feces. There was also goethite, a mineral common to the environment. The burnt rubber likely came from a gasket in the Lux burner Brønlund had among his supplies, and may have been burnt as the doomed explorer struggled to light a fire to keep warm, using any potentially flammable materials available to him. (The burner was recovered in 1973 by the Sirius Patrol.) The authors surmise that Brønlund was in the cave roughly five or six days before he died, given that the sledge case was barely half-empty.
 "This new knowledge gives a unique insight into Brønlund's last hours,"  said Rasmussen. "I see for me, how he, weakened and with dirty, shaking hands, fumbled in an attempt to light the burner, but failed. He had to find something else to get the burner going. You can use paper or oiled fabric, but it is difficult. We think he tried with the oils available, because the black spot contains traces of vegetable oil and oils that may come from fish, animals or wax candles."
title: 研究揭示了100多年后极地探险者的最后工作时间
title_en: Study sheds new light on polar explorer’s final hours, 100+ years later

富士胶片宣布，它已经创造了一项新的世界纪录，它创造了一种可以存储惊人的580 TB数据的磁存储带。该突破与IBM Research共同开发，使用了一种称为锶铁氧体（SrFe）的新型磁性颗粒，该颗粒通常用作制造电动机磁体的原料。富士胶片一直在研究锶铁氧体，以取代目前最主要的钡铁氧体（BaFe）。
  已发现SrFe具有比BaFe更高的磁特性和60％的颗粒体积。与BaFe一样，SrFe是化学稳定的氧化物，这意味着它对于长期数据保存应表现良好。
  Fujifilm说：“ SrFe是一种磁性材料，具有很高的磁性，即使加工成细颗粒也能稳定地保持高性能。” “该测试中使用的磁带是在该公司现有的涂层设备上生产的，这证明了支持大规模生产和商业化的能力。”
 磁带上的数据以创纪录的密度（每平方英寸317 GB）存储，这要归功于IBM开发的新技术，包括新的低摩擦带头技术以及其背后的执行器和伺服器。头部的速度和准确性非常出色。
 “请稍等一下，” IBM研究经理Mark Lantz写道。 “当读取磁带时，它以约15 km / h的速度流过磁头，使用我们的新伺服技术，我们仍然能够以大约DNA分子宽度的1.5倍的精度来定位磁头。 。”
 新磁带的最终数据存储容量大约是当今市场上现有磁带的50倍。
  要透视580 TB，大约相当于120,000张DVD或786,977张CD – IBM指出，堆叠许多CD会导致塔高3,097英尺（944m），甚至比世界上最高的建筑Burj Kalifa高。 
现在，所有这些数据都可以放入手掌中的盒式磁带中。  IBM写道：“就成本而言，在磁带上存储数据是每千兆字节1美分，而在不使用时，磁带不需要像硬盘和闪存那样的能源。” 简单地说，磁带存储的数据可确保云提供商在需要时即可获得所需的数据。  “此外，如果存储得当，今天记录在磁带上的数据将在30年后仍可被读取。”
note_en:
Fujifilm  has announced that it has set a new world record by creating a magnetic storage tape that can store a staggering  580 terabytes of data.  The breakthrough, developed jointly with IBM Research, uses a new magnetic particle called Strontium Ferrite (SrFe), commonly used as a raw material for making motor magnets. Fujifilm has been investigating Strontium Ferrite as a possible successor to Barium Ferrite (BaFe), which is the leading material today.
  SrFe has been found to have higher magnetic characteristics and 60% less particle volume than BaFe. And like BaFe, SrFe is a chemically stable oxide, meaning it should perform well for long-term data preservation.
  “SrFe is a magnetic material that has very high magnetic properties and is stable to maintain high performance even when processed into fine particles,” Fujifilm says. “Magnetic tapes used in this test have been produced at the company’s existing coating facility, confirming the ability to support mass production and commercialization.”
 Data on the tape is stored at a record-breaking density of 317 gigabytes per square inch thanks to new technologies developed by IBM, including a new low friction tape head technology and the actuators and servos behind it. The head’s speed and accuracy are remarkable.
 “Just let me geek out for a second,” writes IBM research manager Mark Lantz. “When tape is being read it is streamed over the head at a speed of about 15 km/h and with our new servo technologies we are still able to position the tape head with an accuracy that is about 1.5 times the width of a DNA molecule.”
 The resulting data storage capacity of the new tapes is roughly 50 times greater than the current cartridges on the market today.
  To put 580 terabytes in perspective, it’s roughly the equivalent of 120,000 DVDs or 786,977 CDs — IBM notes that stacking that many CDs would result in a tower 3,097 feet (944m) tall, or taller than Burj Kalifa, the world’s tallest building.
 All that data can now fit in a tape cartridge in the palm of your hand.
  “In terms of costs, storing data on tape is pennies per gigabyte and when not in use, tape requires no energy unlike hard disks and flash,” IBM  writes. “Put simply, tape-stored data ensures that cloud providers will have the data they need when they need it.
 “In addition, when stored properly, data recorded on tape today will still be readable in 30 years.”
title: Fujifilm创建了可以存储580 TB的磁带
title_en: Fujifilm Created a Magnetic Tape That Can Store 580 Terabytes

在构建名为stagetimer.io的事件计时器应用程序时，我遇到了显示时间的异常现象，发现iPhone计时器通过向我们显示假时间来解决该问题。根据定义，倒数显示剩余时间。因此，如果倒数计时为5秒，我们假设还剩5秒。但这不是全部。
  iPhone的倒数计时器未严格显示正确的时间，但会增加500ms或半秒的剩余时间。这样做可以使人类更直观地阅读时间。倒计时结束时的警报不受此500ms错误的影响。
  Javascript在处理时间时喜欢使用毫秒，1000ms等于1s。这是一个5秒倒计时的示例，该倒计时从5000毫秒开始，并使用setInterval（）函数每10毫秒减去10毫秒，这非常简单。通过除以1000并四舍五入将毫秒数转换为秒，如下所示：Math.floor（milliseconds / 1000）
  到达开始时，计时器会立即跳到4s，一旦计时器切换到0s，仍然还有1s。这在向上计数时非常有意义，例如，在上午10点的第一分钟显示10:00，而不是10:01，始终四舍五入。但是对于倒数计时器，这是违反直觉的。计时器是否显示小数秒更容易理解。
  现在，计时器显示的是0.9s秒而不是0s，以清楚地显示时钟上还有剩余时间。但是，我不想为计时器显示小数秒。
  所以现在我很好奇我的iPhone如何解决这个难题。因此，我将iPhone计时器设置为5s：
  单击“开始”后，iPhone计时器显示5s，而不是上面示例中的4s。但它会在整整一秒钟到期之前切换到4s。然后，它会计数适当的秒数，直到达到0s，这又不是整秒。而且，如果您在“暂停”跳到0s之后立即点击，它将立即跳回到1s，以显示实际上倒计时还剩下一些时间。 
我认为，苹果公司的好伙伴在实际时间上增加了500ms的伪造时间，以5s而不是4s开始倒计时显示。 如果实际时间达到0s，“假”时间达到500ms，计时器结束并且电话发出蜂鸣声。 因此他们遇到了我遇到的相同问题，并提出了一个切实可行的解决方案。 毕竟，如果您开始倒数计时5s，则应该从5s开始计时，对吧？ 为了说明，这是我的简单计时器执行的相同操作。  如果这篇文章对您有帮助或有任何疑问，我希望收到您的来信。  推特
note_en:
While building my event timer app called  stagetimer.io I came across a peculiarity with displaying time found out that the iPhone timer addresses it by showing us a fake time. By definition, a countdown shows how much time is left. So if the countdown says 5s we assume there are 5 seconds left. But that’s not the whole truth.
  The iPhone countdown timer doesn’t strictly display the correct time but adds 500ms, or half a second, to the remaining time. It does this to make the reading of time more intuitive for humans. The alarm at the end of the countdown is not affected by this 500ms inaccuracy.
  Javascript likes to use milliseconds when dealing with time, 1000ms equals 1s. Here is an example of a 5s countdown that starts at 5000ms and uses the  setInterval() function to deduct 10ms every 10ms, simple enough. Milliseconds are converted to seconds by dividing by 1000 and rounding down like so:  Math.floor(milliseconds / 1000)
  The timer jumps to 4s right when hitting start and once the timer switches to 0s there are still 1s to go. This makes a lot of sense when counting up, for example, 10:00 is displayed during the first minute of 10 AM, not 10:01, always rounding down. But for a countdown timer, this is counterintuitive. It is easier to understand if the timer has a fractional seconds display.
  Now the timer displays 0.9s seconds instead of 0s to show clearly that there is still time left on the clock. However, I didn’t want to show fractional seconds for my timer.
  So now I was curious how my iPhone solves this conundrum. So I set my iPhone timer to 5s:
  After I click “Start” the iPhone timer shows 5s, not 4s like in the example above. But it switches to 4s before a full second expired. It then counts proper seconds until it reaches 0s which, again, is not a full second. And if you tap “Pause” just after it jumped to 0s it will promptly jump back to 1s to show you that there is, in fact, still some time left on the countdown.
 I figured that the good folks at Apple add an extra fake 500ms to the actual time to start that countdown display at 5s instead of 4s. The timer ends and the phone beeps if the actual time hits 0s and the “fake” time hits 500ms. So they faced the same problem I did and came up with a practical solution. After all, if you start a 5s countdown, it should start at 5s right? For illustration, here is my simple timer doing the same trick.
 I would love to hear from you if this article was helpful or if you have any questions
 Twitter
title: 为什么iPhone Timer App显示假时间
title_en: Why the iPhone Timer App displays a Fake Time

note_en:
Apple has started accepting applications for a new mentorship program that aims to help first-generation college students. In a  PDF obtained by   MacRumors, the company details Launch@Apple. The document invites immigrant college freshmen and sophomores who are majoring in finance, economics, accounting or a discipline related to business, mathematics, commerce or data analytics to apply for the chance to get one one-on-one time with Apple mentors. The document notes they’ll also be opportunities for job shadowing, as well as paid internships and externships.
 The program is limited to students who have a parent or guardian who has not obtained their own college degree. The company is also looking for individuals who are “eager to learn about working in finance in a fast-paced, innovative environment.” Apple is accepting applications until Friday, January 8th, 2021. To apply, students need to answer 18 questions that cover topics like their legal status in the US, GPA and aspirations, among other personal information.
  We reached out to Apple for more information on the program. We’ll update this article when we hear back from the company.
title: 苹果悄悄启动了一对一的大学指导计划

note:
关于民主的一件好事是，至少在理论上，我们不需要自由和私下发表言论的许可。我们不必在线或离线证明自己的讲话符合政府的标准。我们不必“赢得”言论自由或隐私权。
 时代变了。如今，一些美国参议员认为，在线世界中的演讲是一种例外情况，在这种情况下，网站所有者需要“赚钱”，无论他们打算谨慎管理用户内容还是让用户自由发言。 2020年，两位参议员提出了一项旨在限制在线语音和安全性的法案，名为“ EARN IT Act”，该法案的首字母缩写也是“消除对交互式技术的滥用和肆意忽视”。
 参议员林赛·格雷厄姆（R-SC）和理查德·布卢门撒（Richard Blumenthal）（D-CT）以侵害儿童罪为借口，为在线演讲的重要法律保护打了个洞，共同提出了这项法律。最初的《 EARN IT法案》创建了一个由19人组成的政府委员会，并为执法人员保留了席位，这将为在线平台创建“最佳实践”。这不仅仅针对像Facebook这样的大型网站-新规则将适用于本地新闻网站，业余博客和电子邮件服务以及其他在线服务。任何不遵循“最佳做法”的人都将失去重要的法律保护，并可能因使用其服务的人员的行为而承担责任或被起诉。
 很明显，执法机构希望互联网公司和网站托管商采用哪种做法。美国总检察长威廉·巴尔（William Barr）反复说过一遍，他认为，执法机构应该始终可以访问加密通信。但是，正如我们一遍又一遍地解释的那样，使用“后门”进行加密只是破坏性加密。无论您将访问加密的方式称为“客户端扫描”还是“端点过滤”，或其他任何方式都没有关系。后门也不只是被好人所使用。威权政府和罪犯总是对阅读别人的信息感兴趣。
 EARN IT Act的结构旨在允许执法人员监视Internet语音。它将使执法机构，从联邦调查局到地方警察，都可以扫描在线发送的每条消息。任何未授予执法人员合法访问任何数字消息（肯定是委员会授权的“最佳做法”）的公司都将受到诉讼甚至刑事诉讼。如果该法案获得通过，该法案将对隐私和言论自由造成毁灭性的打击。这是因为，如果没有责任保护，网站将审查和规范用户的语音，甚至完全取消某些语音类别。
 但是最初的法案甚至没有提前退出委员会，因为公众对此的强烈抗议。所提议的法律非常不受欢迎，因此对《 EARN IT法》进行了修改，以减少政府委员会的权力。 EARN IT Act的新版本并没有建立由执法部门主导的权威委员会，而是建立了相同的委员会，只是使其成为“顾问”。取而代之的是，该法案赋予了规范州立法机关互联网的权力。
 经修订的《 EARN IT法案》并没有比原始版本更好，并且在某些方面甚至更糟。只要名义上的目的是制止虐待儿童的在线行为，它就可以以他们想要的任何方式为美国50个州以及美国领土的立法机关提供广泛的泊位。参议院司法委员会还通过了一项修正案，旨在保护端到端加密不受各州的侵犯。这项修正案是对这次监视法案所引发的对加密命运的担忧的充分肯定，但这还远远不够。 
除了加密以外，这里的另一个真正目标是第230节（《美国法典》第47条第230款），该法律被错误地恶化为大企业提供特殊保护。实际上，第230节保护了日常互联网用户的讲话和安全。
 国会议员们在2020年的最后几个月里提出了有关如何削弱第230条的想法，而联邦法律根本没有违反。 《 PACT法案》旨在解决最大的在线平台的主导地位，但最终将审查用户并巩固最大的平台的主导地位。 2020年末的另一项提案是《在线内容政策现代化法案》，完全是违反宪法的。
 这些想法将在2021年继续流行。对大技术的批评是合理的，但是它却导致许多政客误导了人们对在线语音的控制，或者赋予了警察更多的控制和监视互联网的权力。如果对第230节提出错误建议的提议通过，则附带损害可能会很严重。
 我们都知道在线世界中存在严重的问题，例如虚假信息，可恶的言论和侵蚀隐私的行为。总体而言，重大问题与第230节无关，该节仍然可以实现其保护用户语音的目标。之所以存在大问题，是因为少数高科技公司拥有太多权力。巨型科技公司的行为越来越像垄断。他们的内容审核系统已损坏。
 我们确实需要针对这些问题的新解决方案。而且他们在那里：我们需要考虑加强反托拉斯执法，改革CFAA等反竞争法律，制定强大的隐私法规，通过使其可移植性来释放用户数据以及创造竞争兼容性的机会。
 本文是“年度回顾”系列的一部分。阅读有关2020年争取数字权利的其他文章。
note_en:
One nice thing about democracy is that—at least in theory—we don’t need permission to speak freely and privately. We don’t have to prove that our speech meets the government’s criteria, online or offline. We don’t have to “earn” our rights to free speech or privacy.
 Times have changed. Today, some U.S. senators have come to the view that speech in the online world is an exceptional case, in which website owners need to “earn it”—whether they intend to carefully moderate user content, or let users speak freely. In 2020, two Senators introduced a bill that would limit speech and security online, titled the EARN IT Act, which is also an acronym that stands for “Eliminating Abusive and Rampant Neglect of Interactive Technologies.”
 Using crimes against children as an excuse to blow a hole in critical legal protections for online speech, Senators Lindsey Graham (R-SC) and Richard Blumenthal (D-CT) co-sponsored this law. The  original EARN IT Act created a 19-person government commission, stacked with seats reserved for law enforcement, that would create “best practices” for online platforms to follow. This wouldn’t have just targeted big websites like Facebook—the new rules would apply to a local news websites, hobby blogs, and email services, among other online services. Anyone who didn’t follow the “best practices” would lose critical legal protections and could be held liable, or prosecuted, for the actions of the people who use their services.
 It’s clear what practices law enforcement want Internet companies and website hosts to adopt. U.S. Attorney General William Barr has said it repeatedly—in his view,  law enforcement agencies should always have access to encrypted communications. But as we’ve explained over and over again, encryption with a “backdoor” is  just broken encryption. It doesn’t matter if you call the means of accessing encryption “ client side scanning” or “endpoint filtering” or anything else. Backdoors don’t just get used by good guys, either. Authoritarian governments and criminals are always interested in reading other peoples’ messages.
 The structure of the EARN IT Act was designed to allow Internet speech to be monitored by law enforcement. It would have let law enforcement agencies, from the FBI down to local police, to  scan every message sent online . Any company that didn’t grant law enforcement legal access to any digital message (a “best practice” sure to have been mandated by the commission) would be subject to lawsuits or even criminal prosecutions. If it had passed, the bill would have been devastating to both privacy and free expression. That’s because without liability protections, websites will censor and regulate user speech, or even eliminate certain categories for speech altogether.
 But the original bill didn’t even advance out of committee—because public outcry against it was overwhelming. The law as proposed was so unpopular, the EARN IT Act was amended to reduce the power of the government commission. Rather than creating an authoritative law enforcement-dominated commission, the new version of the EARN IT Act establishes the same commission, but simply makes it “advisory.” Instead, the bill gives the power to regulate the Internet to state legislatures.
 This amended EARN IT Act  isn’t any better than the original, and in some ways is even worse. It gives wide berth to legislatures in all 50 states, as well as U.S. territories, to regulate the Internet in just about any way they want—as long as the nominal purpose is to stop the online abuse of children. The Senate Judiciary Committee also passed an amendment that purported to protect end-to-end encryption from being violated by the states. That amendment was a worthy nod to the outpouring of concern for the fate of encryption that this surveillance bill had prompted, but it doesn’t go far enough.
  Looking beyond encryption, the other real target here was Section 230 (47 U.S.C. §230), a law that has been falsely maligned as providing special protection to Big Tech companies. In reality,  Section 230 protects the speech and security of everyday Internet users.
 Lawmakers in Congress filled the final months of 2020 with ideas about how to weaken Section 230, a federal law that is  simply not broken. The PACT Act was an effort to address the dominance of the biggest online platforms, but would end up  censoring users and entrenching the dominance of the biggest platforms. Another late 2020 proposal, the Online Content Policy Modernization Act, was  simply an unconstitutional mess.
 These ideas are going to keep coming back in 2021. There’s  justifiable criticism of Big Tech, but it’s led many politicians into misguided attempts to control online speech, or give police more power to control and surveil the Internet. If a wrongheaded proposal to gut Section 230 passes, the collateral damage could be severe.
 We all know there are serious problems in the online world, like disinformation, hateful speech, and  eroded privacy. By and large,  the big problems aren’t related to Section 230, which still fulfills its goal of protecting user speech. The big problems exist because a few tech companies have too much power. Giant tech companies act increasingly like  monopolies . Their content moderation systems are  broken.
 We do need new solutions to these problems. And they’re out there: we need to think about beefing up  antitrust enforcement, reforming  anti-competitive laws like the CFAA, creating  strong privacy regulations, freeing user data by making it  portable, and creating opportunities for  competitive compatibility.
 This article is part of our Year in Review series.    Read other articles about the fight for digital rights in 2020.
title: 2020年，国会通过“获取IT”法案威胁到我们的言论和安全
title_en: In 2020, Congress Threatened Our Speech and Security with the “Earn IT” Act

note:
抒情作家巴里·洛佩兹（Barry Lopez）星期五沉迷于俄勒冈州尤金的家中，他沉浸在北极荒野，狼群和异国风光的栖息地中，以探索自然与人类文化的殊荣的获奖书籍。是75。
 他的妻子黛布拉（Debra Gwartney）确认死亡，并说洛佩兹先生患有前列腺癌。她说，自从9月以来，他们的家庭就一直住在尤金（Eugene）的临时房屋内，此前他们在俄勒冈州芬恩罗克（Finn Rock）附近的麦肯齐河（McKenzie River）的长期住所被野火烧毁。
 在半个世纪的旅行中，洛佩兹先生到80个国家/地区旅行了近几十种非小说和小说作品，包括大量的散文和短篇小说，洛佩兹先生将山水和文学与人道主义，环境和精神的情感相提并论，有些批评家将其比作梭罗和约翰·缪尔。
 他凭借“北极之梦”（1986年）获得了国家图书奖（非小说类作品），这是他与因纽特人在一起五年的经历，他在一片寒冷而无尽的土地上独居。在那儿，他发现how叫的风暴可以造就海市ages楼-猎人缠着一只灰熊，当他走近时，变成一只土拨鼠，或者一只长着翅膀飞走的北极熊：只有一只白雪皑皑的猫头鹰。
 Kakutani在《纽约时报》的“北极梦”评论中写道：“它把遥远的冰雪世界视为一个不仅存在于地理数学中，而且还存在于我们想象中的土地中的地方。” 。 “对于洛佩兹先生来说，这是一个'飞机跟踪克利夫兰大小的冰山，北极熊从天上飞下来的土地，'这片土地上充满了意象和隐喻。”
  在加拿大北极地区，洛佩兹先生曾经被一场如此强烈的暴风雪所吞噬，这变成了一种神秘的经历：三维空间似乎在他周围消失了。他将其科学解释为一种轻巧的技巧，但在狂想曲散文中描述了这种现象及其更深层的含义。
 他写道：“没有阴影。” “空间没有深度。没有视野。世界的底部消失了。徒步时，您会以错失的阶梯方式绊倒。正是因为北极的光和时间的不同，使得这种景观能够以惊人的方式暴露出我们对土地的总体思想的自满。” 
洛佩兹先生从小就受罗马天主教的熏陶和教育，他认为职业是祭司或流浪汉。但是，他决定当一名作家，于是在2017年的一次ob告中接受采访时解释说，他离开了教堂，从1960年代末开始，他对自然及其对人类的影响产生了深深的敬意。
 当被问及写作动机时，他说：“我可以用两个字告诉你。” “帮助。我是传统讲故事的人。此活动与您无关。这与文化有关，您的工作就是提供帮助。”
  英国作家罗伯特·麦克法伦（Robert Macfarlane）在2005年的《卫报》中这样说：“洛佩兹在他的著作中始终坚持认为自然景观能够赋予穿越自然景观的人们以恩典的思想。在他看来，某些景观形式具有精神上的对应。陡峭的山坡曲线，沙滩上潮湿的石头巢，风吹树的弯曲树干：这些抽象的形状可以在我们身上呼唤我们可能不知道的善良。”
  1976年，洛佩斯先生前往阿拉斯加旅行，为一家杂志研究狼，然后专门研究狼的历史，知识，栖息地和文学。 。他采访了美国和加拿大西北部地区的科学家，捕兽者和土著人民。他甚至养了一只狼幼崽。
 他的著作《狼与人》（1978年）是美国国家图书奖的决赛入围者，并获得了约翰·伯劳斯奖章和克里斯托弗奖。在人类与狼的关系史上，它把事实与虚构相分离，批评家称之为对这种动物进行了明眼的评估，这种动物在欧洲和北美地区被迷惑地替罪了，历史上几乎被灭绝。
 惠特利·斯特里伯（Whitley Strieber）在《华盛顿邮报》的一篇评论中说：“在了解我们对狼的了解与想象之间的差异时，洛佩兹阐明了一些有关人类经历的痛苦真相。” “面对悲剧，他没有受到任何指责，这使我们对狼所做的一切成为了有关人类的新知识。”
 洛佩兹先生的小说集小说和短篇小说为主，反映出他的人文主义信念，是冒险，亲密，道德和身份的融合。在很久以前的寓言《乌鸦与鼬鼠》（Crow and Weasel，1990）中，“当人类和动物说相同的语言时”，两个年轻人离开了他们的平原部落，成年后在旷野中寻求危险。 
“加勒比海地区的轻型行动”（2000年）是一部短篇小说集，受到洛佩兹先生对自尊的救赎价值的信念的约束。在“ Emory Bear Hands’Birds”中，一位被囚禁的美国原住民讲故事的人使用魔术现实主义来唤起同胞的希望。在标题故事中，一位年轻女子在加勒比海度假天堂中得意洋洋，唯物主义的雅pp喜人：
 “第一枚子弹撕破了他的左肱三头肌，第二枚，第三枚，第四枚和第五枚未击中，第六枚穿孔了他的脾脏，第七枚和第八枚未击中，第九枚击中了控制台，发出了电火花，第十枚击中了他的腹部。右手掌，接下来的四个飞到空中，第十五个撕开左耳，第十六个跳动离开第六个颈椎，穿过他的心脏，穿过他的腹部并停留在他的脚下。”
 Barry Lopez于1945年1月6日出生于纽约州切斯特港的Barry Holstun Brennan，是John和Mary Frances（Holstun）Brennan的两个儿子中的大儿子，他们居住在纽约附近的Mamaroneck，Barry的兄弟Dennis出生。 1948年，一家人搬到了加利福尼亚州的瑞塞达（Reseda），然后搬到了洛杉矶的圣费尔南多谷（San Fernando Valley）的半农村地区。
 1950年离婚后，玛丽·布伦南（Mary Brennan）在高中和一所大专院校教授家庭经济学。 1955年，她与收养儿子的商人艾德里安·伯纳德·洛佩兹（Adrian Bernard Lopez）结婚。他们俩都以洛佩兹姓氏。
 巴里（Barry）的母亲到莫哈维沙漠（Mojave Desert）和大峡谷旅行，激发了他对自然的热爱。他在附近的洛杉矶恩奇诺（Encino）一家罗马天主教小学上学。
 巴里11岁时，一家人搬到曼哈顿，在那里他参加了耶稣会的Loyola学校，并在1962年毕业时担任高级班长。他考虑过担任圣职，但就读于圣母大学，并获得了学士学位。他告诉《泰晤士报》，他于1966年获得通讯学位，并于1968年获得硕士学位。他在肯塔基州的一家修道院度过了一个月，但他认为修道院生活“太过容易了”。
 1967年，他与Sandra Jean Landers结婚。他们于1998年离婚。2007年，他与黛布拉·阿琳·格沃特尼（Debra Arleen Gwartney）结婚，后者育有四个女儿：阿曼达（Amanda），斯蒂芬妮（Stephanie）和玛丽·伍德拉夫（Mary Woodruff）和莫莉·哈格（Mollie Harger）；还有一个同父异母的兄弟约翰·布伦南（John Brennan），他们所有人都得以生存。他的兄弟丹尼斯于2017年去世。 
洛佩兹先生是《哈珀杂志》的特约编辑，并为许多国家出版物撰稿。 他曾在哥伦比亚大学，东华盛顿大学，爱荷华大学，明尼苏达州的卡尔顿学院和得克萨斯理工大学任教，在那里他的作品被存档。  他的论文集“关于这一生：记忆阈值之旅”（1998年）探索了世界各地和心灵中的风景。  萨拉·惠勒（Sara Wheeler）在《泰晤士报》上写道：“洛佩兹不仅将读者带到了另一个地方，也带给了他们自己。” “他对人类社会与自然之间的裂痕非常感兴趣，更具体地说，对艺术可以封闭它的理论非常着迷。 他相信艺术家社区。”
note_en:
Barry Lopez, a lyrical writer who steeped himself in Arctic wildernesses, the habitats of wolves and exotic landscapes around the world for award-winning books that explored the kinship of nature and human culture, died on Friday at his home in Eugene, Ore. He was 75.
 His wife, Debra Gwartney, confirmed his death and said that Mr. Lopez had had prostate cancer. She said the family had been living in a temporary home in Eugene since September, after their longtime home along the McKenzie River, near Finn Rock, Ore.,  was consumed by a wildfire.
 In a half-century of travel to 80 countries that generated nearly a score of nonfiction and fiction works, including volumes of essays and short stories, Mr. Lopez embraced landscapes and literature with humanitarian, environmental and spiritual sensibilities that some critics likened to those of Thoreau and John Muir.
 He won the National Book Award (nonfiction) for “Arctic Dreams” (1986), a treatise on his five years with Inuit people and solitude in a land of bitter cold and endless expanses. There he found that howling storms could craft mirages — a hunter stalking a grizzly bear that, as he approaches, turns into a marmot, or a polar bear that grows wings and flies away: only a snowy owl.
 “It treats the distant snowy world of the Arctic as a place that exists not only in the mathematics of geography, but also in the terra incognita of our imaginations,” Michiko Kakutani wrote in a  review of “Arctic Dreams” in The New York Times. “For Mr. Lopez, it is a land where ‘airplanes track icebergs the size of Cleveland and polar bears fly down out of the stars,’ a land rich in imagery and metaphor.”
  In the Canadian Arctic, Mr. Lopez was once engulfed in a blizzard of such intensity that it turned into a mystical experience: Three-dimensional space seemed to vanish all around him. He explained the science of it as a trick of light, but described the phenomenon and its deeper meaning in rhapsodic prose.
 “There are no shadows,” he wrote. “Space has no depth. There is no horizon. The bottom of the world disappears. On foot, you stumble about in missed stair-step fashion. It is precisely because the regimes of light and time in the Arctic are so different that this landscape is able to expose in startling ways the complacency of our thoughts about land in general.”
 Raised and educated in Roman Catholic traditions, Mr. Lopez as a young man considered vocations as a priest or a Trappist monk. But, deciding to be a writer, he drifted away from the church, as he explained in an interview for this obituary in 2017, and starting in the late 1960s he adopted a deep reverence for nature and its effect upon humanity.
 “I can tell you in two words,” he said when asked about his motives for writing. “To help. I am a traditional storyteller. This activity is not about yourself. It’s about culture, and your job is to help.”
  The British writer Robert Macfarlane put it this way in  The Guardian in 2005: “Throughout his writings, Lopez returns to the idea that natural landscapes are capable of bestowing a grace upon those who pass through them. Certain landscape forms, in his vision, possess a spiritual correspondence. The stern curve of a mountain slope, a nest of wet stones on a beach, the bent trunk of a windblown tree: These abstract shapes can call out in us a goodness we might not have known we possessed.”
  After a trip to Alaska to research wolves for a magazine assignment in 1976, Mr. Lopez devoted two years to a study of the history, lore, habitats and literature of wolves, reviled in myths as evil and hunted since the Dark Ages as bloodthirsty beasts. He interviewed scientists, trappers and native people in the American and Canadian Northwest. He even raised a wolf pup.
 His book “Of Wolves and Men” (1978) was a National Book Award finalist and won the John Burroughs Medal and the Christopher Award. A history of man’s relationship with wolves, it separated fact from fiction in what critics called a cleareyed evaluation of a creature that has been superstitiously scapegoated and historically slaughtered nearly to extinction in regions of Europe and North America.
 “In coming to terms with the difference between what we know and what we imagine about the wolf, Lopez has shed light on some painful truths about the human experience,” Whitley Strieber said in a review for The Washington Post. “By laying no blame while facing the tragedy for what it is, he has made what we have done to the wolf a source of new knowledge about man.”
 Mr. Lopez’s fiction, a shelf of novels and short stories, reflected his humanist convictions, a blend of adventure, intimacy, ethics and identity. In “Crow and Weasel” (1990), a fable of long ago, “when people and animals spoke the same language,” two youngsters leave their plains tribe and come of age facing perils in the wilderness on a quest for wisdom.
 “Light Action in the Caribbean” (2000) was a diverse collection of short stories bound by Mr. Lopez’s belief in the redemptive values of self-respect. In “Emory Bear Hands’ Birds,” an imprisoned Native American storyteller uses magic realism to evoke hope in fellow inmates. In the title story, a young woman gives a smug, materialistic yuppie his comeuppance in a Caribbean vacation paradise:
 “The first bullet tore through his left triceps, the second, third, fourth and fifth hit nothing, the sixth perforated his spleen, the seventh and eighth hit nothing, the ninth hit the console, sending electrical sparks up, the tenth went through his right palm, the next four went into the air, the fifteenth tore his left ear away, the sixteenth ricocheted off the sixth cervical vertebra and drove down through his heart, exiting through his abdomen and lodging in his foot.”
 Barry Lopez was born Barry Holstun Brennan in Port Chester, N.Y., on Jan. 6, 1945, the older of two sons of John and Mary Frances (Holstun) Brennan, who lived in nearby Mamaroneck, N.Y.. Barry’s brother, Dennis, was born in 1948, and the family moved to Reseda, Calif., then a semirural section of Los Angeles in the San Fernando Valley.
 After a divorce in 1950, Mary Brennan taught home economics in high schools and a junior college. In 1955 she married Adrian Bernard Lopez, a businessman who adopted her sons. They both took the Lopez surname.
 Barry’s mother encouraged his love of nature with trips to the Mojave Desert and the Grand Canyon. He attended a Roman Catholic grade school in the nearby Los Angeles neighborhood of Encino.
 When Barry was 11, the family moved to Manhattan, where he attended the Loyola School, a Jesuit institution, and was senior class president, graduating in 1962. He considered the priesthood but enrolled in the University of Notre Dame, where he earned a bachelor’s degree in communications in 1966 and a master’s in teaching in 1968. He spent a month at a monastery in Kentucky but decided that a monastic life would be “too easy,” he told The Times.
 In 1967, he married Sandra Jean Landers. They were divorced in 1998. In 2007, he married Debra Arleen Gwartney, who had four daughters: Amanda, Stephanie and Mary Woodruff and Mollie Harger; and a half brother, John Brennan, all of whom survive. His brother, Dennis, died in 2017.
 Mr. Lopez was a contributing editor for Harper’s Magazine and wrote for many national publications. He taught at Columbia University, Eastern Washington University, the University of Iowa, Carleton College in Minnesota and Texas Tech University, where his works are archived.
 His essay collection “About This Life: Journeys on the Threshold of Memory” (1998) explored landscapes around the world and in the mind.
 “Lopez takes readers not only out of themselves to another place, but into themselves as well,”  Sara Wheeler wrote in The Times. “He is much attracted to the rift that has opened between human society and nature, and, more specifically, to the theory that art can close it. He believes in the community of artists.”
title: 被喻为梭罗的抒情作家巴里·洛佩兹去世，享年75岁
title_en: Barry Lopez, Lyrical Writer Who Was Likened to Thoreau, Dies at 75

Reddit的替代Voat在圣诞节那天关闭，原因是缺乏运营资金，并对其他类似的几乎一切都可以进行的言论自由表示怀疑。平台可以长期保持在线状态。
 我只是不能跟上它， Voat联合创始人贾斯汀·查斯顿（Justin Chastain）在关闭公告中表示。他解释说，2020年3月投资枯竭。 ＆＃34;我个人决定在直到2020年美国大选之后才继续投票。我一直在自掏腰包支付费用，但现在我已经没钱了。＆＃34;
 Voat于2014年首次推出，是一种较小的Reddit替代品，专门用于＆＃34; free语音，＆＃34;包括露骨的仇恨言论，极端右翼内容，种族主义以及其他网站上限制或禁止的其他内容。它在2015年获得了关注，当时Reddit最终从其平台上禁止了几项明显的种族歧视行为，以限制骚扰，一些不满的Reddit用户决定迁移。
 在2015年中，Voat的网络托管服务商Host Europe暂停了对该平台的服务，并表示，我们将不容忍任何形式的非法右翼极端主义内容，并明确表示与这一点保持距离。 ＆＃34; （东道国欧洲总部设在德国，其仇恨言论法律比美国强。）
    由于有人指控该平台托管未成年人的露骨色情图片，PayPal还在2015年暂停了Voat的帐户。暂停后，Voat删除了几个论坛，其中包括专门针对＆＃34; jailbait和＆＃34;的论坛。并转而接受比特币等数字货币的捐赠。
 到2020年，从其他平台启动的另一波用户已迁移到Voat：QAnon拥护者。 QAnon阴谋与现实世界中的几起暴力事件有关。 Reddit在2018年禁止了几个与Q相关的社区，Twitter，Facebook和YouTube在2020年也效仿了。
  其他几个所谓的“言论自由”主流社交媒体公司试图保持最恶劣的仇恨言论和暴力威胁，使其逃离自己的网站，因此在过去几年中也推出了该平台。到目前为止，这些努力是混合的。例如，Gab于2016年推出，只是成为新纳粹和反犹太人的避风港，包括在2018年在匹兹堡犹太教堂犯下大规模杀人案的射手。苹果和谷歌都将Gab赶出了各自的移动应用商店; Gab现在使用Mastodon作为平台，使其具有移动性。 
最近，Parler进入了竞争。 在Voat和Gab可能被设计为偏少党派但偏激极端主义者的情况下，Parler并不一定是极端主义者，而是偏重偏激党派的，基于其他社交媒体网站“审查员”的虚假说法，吸引了大多数保守派和右翼用户。 由于政治原因的保守观点。 该网站在整个夏季和秋季都受到热烈欢迎，但在11月大选后，参与度大大下降。
note_en:
Reddit alternative Voat shut down on Christmas Day, citing a lack of operational funding, and casting doubt on the abilities of other similar almost-anything-goes, "free speech" platforms to stay online in the long run.
 "I just can't keep it up," Voat cofounder Justin Chastain  said in the shutdown announcement. Investment dried up in March 2020, he explained. "I personally decided to keep Voat up until after the U.S. election of 2020. I’ve been paying the costs out of pocket but now I’m out of money."
 Voat first launched in 2014 as  a smaller Reddit alternative dedicated to "free speech," including explicit hate speech, extreme right-wing content, racism, and other content limited or prohibited on other sites. It gained traction in 2015, when Reddit  finally banned several explicitly racist subreddits from its platform in a bid to limit harassment, and some discontented Reddit users decided to migrate over.
 In the middle of 2015, Voat's web host, Host Europe,  suspended service to the platform, saying, "we will not tolerate any form of illegal right-wing extremist content and we explicitly distance ourselves from this." (Host Europe is based in Germany, which has  stronger  hate speech laws than the US.)
    PayPal also  suspended Voat's account in 2015, amid allegations the platform was hosting sexually explicit images of minors. Voat removed several forums in the wake of the suspension, including the ones dedicated to "jailbait," and switched to accepting donations in digital currencies such as bitcoin.
 By 2020, another wave of users booted off other platforms had migrated to Voat:  QAnon adherents. The QAnon conspiracy is linked to  several incidents of real-world violence. Reddit  banned several Q-linked communities in 2018, and  Twitter,  Facebook, and  YouTube followed suit in 2020.
  Several other so-called "free speech" platforms have also launched in the past few years, as mainstream social media companies try to keep the worst of the hate speech and violent threats they can catch off their own sites. These efforts, so far, have been mixed. Gab, for example,  launched in 2016 only to become  an immediate haven for neo-Nazis and anti-Semites, including the shooter  who committed mass murder at a Pittsburgh synagogue in 2018. Apple and Google both kicked Gab out of their respective mobile app stores; Gab now  uses Mastodon as a platform, allowing it to have a mobile presence.
  More recently,  Parler has entered the fray. Where Voat and Gab were perhaps designed to be less partisan but more extremist, Parler is not necessarily extremist but skews heavily partisan, drawing mostly conservative and right-wing users based on  false claims that other social media sites "censor" conservative views for political reasons. The site soared in popularity over the summer and into the fall but engagement  largely dropped off in the wake of the November election.
title: 仇恨言论和QAnon的所在地Reddit克隆Voat已关闭
title_en: Reddit clone Voat, home to hate speech and QAnon, has shut down

最终确定的新规则的全文可在此处找到。简而言之，无人机操作员将采用以下三种方法之一：
 1.操作标准的远程ID无人机，该无人机广播无人机和控制站的标识和位置信息；
  2.使用带有远程ID广播模块（可以是连接到无人机的单独设备）的无人机操作，该模块广播标识，位置和起飞信息；要么
 尽管某些无人机操作员可能会受到其他法规的拖延，但鉴于无人机采用的数量和速度之快，它的到来是可以理解的。美国联邦航空局表示，在美国已经注册了超过170万架无人机，以及大约203,000架无人机飞行员的证书。随着更多无人机用于商业目的，这些数字可能只会滚雪球。
 值得注意的是，FAA将新规则视为加快美国无人机交付的一种方法。“新规则通过解决安全和安保问题为将无人机进一步整合到我们的空域中铺平了道路，” FAA管理员史蒂夫·迪克森在发布的新闻稿中表示新闻。 “它们使我们更接近一天，我们将更加常规地看到无人机操作，例如包裹的交付。”
 另一个新名称是“夜间人员操作和夜间操作”规则，顾名思义，该规则同时规定了人员飞行和夜间飞行的能力。该规则具有多种不同的合规性条件，包括重量小于0.55磅的高空飞行。
 根据该规则，“小型无人驾驶飞机不得对人体造成等于或大于由刚性物体撞击时转移25英尺磅动能引起的伤害严重程度的伤害，任何裸露的旋转部件都可能在与人碰撞时划伤人体皮肤，并且没有任何安全缺陷。” 
为了在夜间飞行，无人驾驶飞机需要佩戴可行驶三英里的防撞灯。 该规则将于下个月正式发布，并于60天后正式生效。 无人机制造商将有一年半的时间开始向其设备添加远程ID。 8月，美国联邦航空局（FAA）批准了亚马逊的交付试验许可。
note_en:
The full text of the finalized new rule can  be found here. In short, drone operators will have one of three methods for complying:
 1. Operate a standard Remote ID drone that broadcasts identification and location information of the drone and control station;
  2. Operate a drone with a Remote ID broadcast module (may be a separate device attached to the drone), which broadcasts identification, location, and take-off information; or
 While some drone operators are likely to be put off by additional regulation, its arrival is understandable given the sheer volume and speed of drone adoption. The FAA says that more than 1.7 million drones have been registered in the U.S., along with around 203,000 certifications for drone pilots. Those numbers will likely only snowball as more drones are deployed for commercial purposes.
 Notably, the FAA sees the new rules as a method for accelerating drone deliveries in the U.S. “The new rules make way for the further integration of drones into our airspace by addressing safety and security concerns,” FAA Administrator Steve Dickson said in a release tied to the news. “They get us closer to the day when we will more routinely see drone operations such as the delivery of packages.”
 Also new is the “Operations Over People and at Night” rule, which, as the name implies, regulates both the ability to fly over people and fly at night. The rule features a number of different qualifications for compliance, including weighing less than 0.55 pounds to fly overhead.
 According to the rule, “small unmanned aircraft must not cause injury to a human being that is equivalent to or greater than the severity of injury caused by a transfer of 25 foot-pounds of kinetic energy upon impact from a rigid object, does not contain any exposed rotating parts that could lacerate human skin upon impact with a human being, and does not contain any safety defects.”
 In order to fly at night, drones need to sport operational anti-collision lights that can been see for three miles. The rules are set to be officially published next month, officially becoming effective 60 days later. Drone makers will have a year and a half to begin adding Remote ID to their devices. In August, the  FAA granted Amazon permission for delivery trials.
title: 新的FAA规则要求无人机使用远程ID
title_en: New FAA rule requires Remote ID for drones

我曾经以Capital One作为主要的实体店。银行已有很多年了。直到最近才与他们有问题。几个月前，我登录发现，除了我自己的银行帐户之外，我还神秘地访问了3个新的银行帐户，总金额超过$ 100,000.00。这非常令人担忧，因为我不确定是否又有人可以访问我的帐户。我立即开车到我的第一首都位置。他们说他们将升级到他们的安全部门。该问题从未解决。一个月前，我打电话给他们的安全部门进行了另一次更新。他们说他们没有这个问题的记录（ugh），但是他们会在2周内解决。现在已经超过2周，该问题尚未解决。 **我确实可以使用别人的钱，而他们似乎没有认真对待这个问题？ **我需要一些指导。我害怕身份盗用，这闻起来很烂...
 更新1：过去一个小时，我一直在与Capital One通话，并从一个部门转移到另一个部门。我认为他们确实开始认真对待这一点。我真的很感谢大家的指导。谢谢！为了预防起见，我也正在将资金从我的帐户中转入二级银行。 Capital One向我保证，其他人没有访问我的帐户的权限，但我想确保安全。你们中的许多人都开玩笑说要与其他帐户搞混，但我不会这样做，因为我不喜欢玩火。如果今天有了Capital One，我将发布另一个更新。否则，我会向你们建议的几个监管机构投诉。
 更新2：我终于被提升为他们的管理人员中的某个人。不管它是什么意思。他们说，从案件正式开庭之日起，这类问题通常需要20个工作日才能解决。他说，我希望这个问题能在未来几天内得到解决，一旦问题解决，我将直接收到他们的答复。我确实记录了案件编号以及与我交谈过的两个主要人员的姓名和部门。我将继续向OCC和/或CFPB投诉。
  更新4：我向CFPB投诉。我需要离开计算机一段时间，以便在听到别人的声音之前不再进行任何更新。谢谢大家的指导！
 更新5：很多人问我是否可以看到其他人的PII。答案是肯定的。我可以打开这些帐户中的每个帐户，查看其名称，地址，消费记录，甚至查看带有支票签名的现金支票的图片。
note_en:
I've used Capital One as my primary 'brick and mortar' bank for many years now. Never had an issue with them until recently. A few months ago I logged in to find that in addition to my own bank accounts, I mysteriously had access to 3 new bank accounts with a total of over $100,000.00. This was very concerning because I wasn't sure if in turn, someone else had access to my accounts. I immediately drove to my Capital One location. They said they would escalate to their security division. The problem was never resolved. A month ago I called their security division for another update. They said that they didn't have record of this issue (ugh), but they would resolve it within 2 weeks. It has now been over 2 weeks and the issue has not been resolved. ** I literally have access to other people's money and they don't seem to be taking this issue seriously?! ** I need some guidance on this. I'm scared of identity theft and this smells rotten...
 Update 1: I've been on the phone with Capital One for the last hour, being transferred from department to department. I do think they are starting to take this seriously though. I really appreciate everyone's guidance. Thank you! I am also in the process of transferring money out of MY accounts into my secondary bank as a precaution. Capital One has assured me that the other people do NOT have access to my accounts, but I want to play it safe. Many of y'all have joked about messing with the other accounts, but I won't do that because I don't like playing with fire. I'll post another update if I get anywhere with Capital One today. Otherwise, I will post a complaint with the several regulatory bodies you guys have suggested.
 Update 2: I was finally escalated to someone in their 'management staff' whatever that means. They said this type of issue typically takes 20 business days to resolve from the time the case was officially opened. He said I should expect the issue to be resolved in the next few days and that I would hear back from them directly once it is closed. I did document the Case Number as well as the names and departments of the two primary people I spoke with. I will go ahead and file a complain with the OCC and/or CFPB.
  Update 4: I filed a complaint with the CFPB. I need to step away from my computer for a while, so no more updates until I hear something from someone. Thank you all for your guidance!
 Update 5: A lot of you have asked whether I can see the other people’s PII. The answer is yes. I can open up each of those accounts, see their names, their address, their spending history, and even look at pictures of their cashed checks with their signatures on them.
title: 我可以访问不是我的银行帐户（第一资本银行）
title_en: I Have Access to Bank Accounts That Are Not Mine (Capital One Bank)

莫斯科（路透社）-周一，俄罗斯的监狱服务给了克里姆林宫批评家阿列克谢·纳瓦尼最后一刻：最后一次从德国飞回并在星期二清晨到莫斯科办公室报到，否则，如果您在该截止日期之后返回，将被判入狱。
  弗拉基米尔·普京总统的主要批评家之一纳瓦尔尼在一次飞机上瘫痪后，于八月份被空运到德国接受治疗，飞机被德国和其他西方国家称为试图与诺维奇克神经毒剂谋杀他。
 俄罗斯表示，没有发现他中毒的证据，并否认与事件有任何牵连。
 联邦监狱管理局（FSIN）周一指控纳瓦尼（Navalny）违反了他仍在服刑的缓刑条款，自2014年以来，他仍在服刑，并逃避了俄罗斯刑事检查当局的监督。
 援引英国医学刊物《柳叶刀》上有关他的治疗方法的文章说，纳瓦尼于9月20日从柏林医院出院，其所谓病征的所有症状已于10月12日消失。
  它说：“因此，被定罪的人没有履行法院所赋予他的所有义务，并且在逃避刑事检查专员的监督。”
 Navalny因一起盗窃案而被判处三年半徒刑，他说这是出于政治动机。他的试用期在12月30日到期。 
监狱部门在周一晚些时候的一份声明中说，它已经召集Navalny向检查当局报告，如果他的涉嫌违反缓刑条款的事实被证明是真实的，则可以将其缓刑改为真实监禁。 。  监狱部门没有提到最后期限，但纳瓦尔尼向他的律师发布了一条消息的屏幕截图，该消息称他必须在周二0900之前返回并出现在莫斯科办公室。  他的女发言人基拉·亚尔米什（Kira Yarmysh）在推特上说，纳瓦尼不可能及时返回，他中毒后仍在疗养，并指控监狱服从克里姆林宫的命令。  “明天他不可能出现在莫斯科刑事检查局。 但是FSIN真的在乎常识吗？ 他们得到了命令，他们正在履行它。”她写道。  克里姆林宫曾表示，纳瓦尼可以随时像其他俄罗斯公民一样自由返回俄罗斯。

MOSCOW (Reuters) - Russia’s prison service on Monday gave Kremlin critic Alexei Navalny a last minute ultimatum: Fly back from Germany at once and report at a Moscow office early on Tuesday morning, or be jailed if you return after that deadline.
  Navalny, one of President Vladimir Putin’s leading critics, was airlifted to Germany for treatment in August after collapsing on a plane in what Germany and other Western nations say was an attempt to murder him with a Novichok nerve agent.
 Russia has said it has seen no evidence he was poisoned and has denied any involvement in the incident.
 The Federal Prison Service (FSIN) on Monday accused Navalny of violating the terms of a suspended prison sentence he is still serving out over a conviction dating from 2014, and of evading the supervision of Russia’s criminal inspection authority.
 Citing an article in the British medical publication The Lancet about his treatment, it said Navalny had been discharged from hospital in Berlin on Sept. 20 and that all symptoms of what it called his illness had vanished by Oct. 12.
  “Therefore the convicted man is not fulfilling all of the obligations placed on him by the court, and is evading the supervision of the Criminal Inspectorate,” it said.
 Navalny is serving out a suspended three-and-a-half-year prison term over a theft case he says was politically-motivated. His probation period expires on Dec. 30.
 The prison service said in a statement late on Monday that it had summoned Navalny to report to the inspection authority and that his suspended sentence could be changed to a real jail term if his suspected violations of the terms of the suspended sentence were proven to be true.
 The prison service mentioned no deadline, but Navalny posted a screenshot of a message to his lawyer which said he had until 0900 on Tuesday to return and show up at a Moscow office.
  His spokeswoman, Kira Yarmysh, said on Twitter, it was impossible for Navalny to return in time, that he was still convalescing after his poisoning, and accused the prison service of acting on orders from the Kremlin.
 “There’s no way he could appear at the Moscow Criminal Inspectorate tomorrow. But does the FSIN really care about common sense? They were given an order, they are fulfilling it,” she wrote.
 The Kremlin has said Navalny is free to return to Russia at any time like any other Russian citizen.
title: 俄罗斯给克里姆林宫批评家纳瓦尔尼最后通:：立即返回或入狱
title_en: Russia gives Kremlin critic Navalny an ultimatum: Return immediately or jail
